pr.title: add current directory to include paths and fix a few headers
pr.author: camthesaxman

summary: Updated the build include configuration to search the current source file directory first, then simplified the include path list and normalized several headers to use angle-bracket project includes. Added minimal root include headers for stddef.h and string.h so code can include <string.h> and get size_t/memset declarations. Also replaced a local __mem.h include in aobj.c with <string.h>, indicating the intended dependency was the standard memset declaration rather than a private memory header.
what_changed: Changed Makefile INCLUDES from a long fixed list of include and source subdirectories to `-i $(*D) -I- -i include -i include/dolphin/ -i include/dolphin/mtx/ -i src`, adding the current file directory via `$(*D)`.
Changed Makefile variable assignment for INCLUDES and CFLAGS from `:=` to `=`, likely so `$(*D)` is expanded in the per-target context rather than at initial make parse time.
Added `include/stddef.h` with an include guard and `typedef unsigned long size_t;`.
Added `include/string.h` with an include guard, inclusion of `<stddef.h>`, and a declaration for `void *memset(void *ptr, int value, size_t num);`.
Normalized several includes from quoted paths containing `include/` or project-relative paths to angle-bracket includes, including `<dolphin/types.h>` and `<global.h>`.
Changed `src/sysdolphin/baselib/aobj.c` to include `<string.h>` and removed the direct include of `__mem.h`.
smart_moves: Using `-i $(*D)` makes local quoted includes resolve relative to the source file's own directory, reducing the need to enumerate every source subdirectory in the Makefile include path.
Adding `-I-` separates quote-include lookup behavior from angle-bracket include lookup, supporting a clearer convention where local headers use quotes and root/project headers use angle brackets.
Replacing the long subdirectory include list with a small set of root include directories makes future file movement and new directories less likely to require Makefile edits.
Providing minimal `stddef.h` and `string.h` stubs in the repository root include directory lets decompiled source use standard-looking C library includes without depending on host system headers.
Switching `aobj.c` from `__mem.h` to `<string.h>` documents that its visible dependency is `memset`, avoiding exposure to a private implementation header when only the standard prototype is needed.
decomp_lessons: Prefer stable include roots plus current-directory lookup over maintaining a growing Makefile list of every source subdirectory.

When adding standard library surface area for decompilation, keep headers minimal and only declare what the code actually needs; here `stddef.h` only provides `size_t`, and `string.h` only declares `memset`.
Use angle brackets for repository-wide include roots such as `<dolphin/types.h>`, `<global.h>`, and `<string.h>` after the include path is configured to support that convention.
Avoid including private or implementation-specific headers such as `__mem.h` when a public standard-style header can provide the required declaration.
Deferred Makefile variable expansion can matter when include paths reference automatic variables like `$(*D)`.
naming_conventions: Standard-style include wrappers use conventional names under `include/`, such as `include/stddef.h` and `include/string.h`.
Header guards in the new root include headers use double-underscore all-caps forms: `__STDDEF_H__` and `__STRING_H__`.
Project root includes were normalized to angle brackets, for example `<dolphin/types.h>` instead of `"include/dolphin/types.h"` and `<global.h>` instead of `"include/global.h"`.
Existing local headers in source subdirectories remain quoted, for example `"aobj.h"`, `"jobj.h"`, `"functions.h"`, and `"sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h"`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly or instruction-level matching tactic was shown in the PR excerpt.
The build-system tactic supports matching work indirectly by making compiler include resolution more consistent and less dependent on ad hoc relative paths.
Minimal standard header declarations help avoid pulling in host compiler declarations or extra definitions that could perturb compilation.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rely on `-i $(*D)` or adjust include path behavior after PR 137.
Search for remaining includes using `"include/` prefixes that should be converted to angle-bracket root includes.
Search for remaining direct includes of private memory headers such as `__mem.h` where `<string.h>` would be sufficient.
Check whether later standard headers expanded `include/stddef.h` or `include/string.h` beyond `size_t` and `memset`.
